headline: Croatia FIRST BASELINE — Published 48.4 Functional. 2026 Liberties Rule-of-Law Report classified Croatia among 'Dismantlers' (alongside Bulgaria, Hungary, Italy, Slovakia). Two-thirds of judicial reform recommendations show no progress. Attacks on journalists; insufficient SLAPP protection. Growing political pressure on independent institutions. Net assessment: confirm at 48.4 with internal -2.0 dimensional pressure (sub-threshold; downward carry-forward). The 'Dismantler' classification is significant negative evidence but does not alone exceed material proposal threshold.

internal_reconstructed_dimensions_with_c.carry_forward_disposition: Per v1.2 bilateral application, -2.0 dimensional pressure preserved as downward carry-forward.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3 unchanged at published. Internal -0.1: attacks on journalists reduce signal-amplification.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3 unchanged. Judicial reform recommendations show no progress on 2/3.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3 unchanged. Political pressure on independent institutions documented but no scope-clarity collapse.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3 unchanged at published. Internal -0.2: AB3 Transparency: insufficient SLAPP protection reduces transparency capacity.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3 unchanged at published. Internal -0.2: 'Dismantler' classification = S4 Structural Critique gap (sustaining structures that perpetuate suffering).
scoring_rationale.INT: 3 unchanged at published. Internal -0.2: stated EU rule-of-law commitments vs. Liberties Dismantler classification.

key_evidence: 2026 Liberties Rule-of-Law Report: Croatia classified among 'Dismantlers' (alongside Bulgaria, Hungary, Italy, Slovakia).
Two-thirds of judicial reform recommendations show no progress.
Growing political pressure on independent institutions.

evidence.source: Liberties — Rule of Law Report 2026 press release
evidence.finding: Croatia classified among Dismantlers; comprehensive ROL deterioration documented.

evidence.source: CMS HR — Liberties Rule of Law Report 2026 Croatia coverage
evidence.finding: Stagnation, regression, shrinking space for democracy.

math_check.validator_check: First baseline at 48.4 Functional. -2.0 downward dimensional pressure logged per v1.2.

negativeSignals: 'Dismantler' Liberties classification; 2/3 judicial reform recommendations stalled; journalist attacks; SLAPP protection insufficient; political pressure on independent institutions.
positiveSignals: EU rule-of-law framework continues to apply (external accountability mechanism); some judicial reform progress (1/3) acknowledged.
sector_context: EU / rule-of-law-erosion cluster. Croatia joins Bulgaria, Hungary, Italy, Slovakia as Dismantler. The cluster has notable internal variation (Hungary 28.1->37.5 Developing; Croatia 48.4 Functional).
methodology_consistency_check: First baseline at published score; downward carry-forward pressure preserved per v1.2. Methodologically consistent.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
